atoms.semantic_claim: The downstream diff for crates/ritual-block-verification/src/lib.rs introduces behavior or wiring that must be accounted for in the v2.2 merge target.

destinations.snippet: pub fn verify_short_async_block_with_simulators(...) -> Result<(), BlockVerificationError> { ... verify_spc_calls(block, parent_state)?; verify_nonce_locks(block, parent_state)?; }
destinations.evidence: The v2.2 crate exposes function-based verifier entry points and public helper types instead of the old `BlockVerifier` trait-object wrapper.
Nodebuilder supplies the EVM replay and predicate-evaluation hooks that the old in-crate `BlockVerifier` owned, consuming the v2.2 function API directly.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The old `BlockVerifier`/`BlockVerifierTrait` public wrapper is not carried forward verbatim. In the v2.2 architecture, block verification is exposed as function-based hooks from `ritual-block-verification`, while nodebuilder owns the EVM replay and post-execution validator integration. This preserves the verifier behavior without retaining the old trait-object API surface.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run nodebuilder/Reth post-execution validation on a block containing async settlement/SPC data and assert `verify_short_async_block_with_simulators` rejects malformed SPC proofs while accepting the happy path.
atoms.notes: Resolved the blocked atom by identifying the v2.2 function API and nodebuilder integration that replace the old trait-object wrapper.
